=== SECTION 2: FRONTEND CORE ===
=== FILE: index.html ===
     1: <!doctype html>
     2: <html lang="en">
     3: <head>
     4:   <meta charset="utf-8" />
     5:   <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1" />
     6:   <base href="/" />
     7:   <script>
     8:     // Root access must always load the login page.
     9:     // Keep the SPA router intact for /dashboard, /members, etc.
    10:     // This runs before any heavy assets load to avoid UI flashes.
    11:     (function(){
    12:       try {
    13:         const p = String(location.pathname || '/').toLowerCase();
    14:         if (p === '/' || p === '/login' || p === '/login/' || p === '/index' || p === '/index/') {
    15:           location.replace('/login.html');
    16:         }
    17:       } catch (_) {}
    18:     })();
    19:   </script>
    20:   <title>MUMS | MUMS User Management System</title><link rel="stylesheet" href="/css/styles.css?v=p1-516" />
    21:   <link rel="stylesheet" href="/css/mailbox.css?v=p1-516" />
    22:   <link rel="stylesheet" href="/css/enterprise_ux.css?v=p1-516" />
    23: 
    24:   <style id="enterprise-glass-globals">
    25:     /* Glass Modals Base */
    26:     #settingsModal .panel, #themeModal .panel, #systemCheckModal .panel {
    27:       background: linear-gradient(145deg, rgba(15,23,42,0.85), rgba(2,6,23,0.95)) !important;
    28:       backdrop-filter: blur(24px) !important;
    29:       -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(24px) !important;
    30:       border: 1px solid rgba(56,189,248,0.25) !important;
    31:       box-shadow: 0 30px 60px -12px rgba(0,0,0,0.8), inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.1) !important;
    32:     }

   809: 
   810:   <script src="/js/env_runtime.js?v=p1-516"></script>
   811:   <script src="/js/remote_patch.js?v=p1-516"></script>
   812:   <script>
   813:     // Load Supabase UMD as a first-party script when possible.
   814:     //
   815:     // Edge InPrivate / Tracking Prevention can block storage access for 3rd-party
   816:     // CDN scripts. Serving the SDK from our own origin avoids that.
   817:     (function () {
   818:       const sources = [
   819:         // Cloudflare Pages (PROD)
   820:         '/functions/vendor/supabase.js',
   821:         // Vercel (UAT)
   822:         '/api/vendor/supabase.js',
   823:         // Last-resort fallback
   824:         '[https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@supabase/supabase-js@2.49.0/dist/umd/supabase.min.js](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@supabase/supabase-js@2.49.0/dist/umd/supabase.min.js)'
   825:       ];
   826: 
   827:       function loadAt(i) {
   828:         if (i >= sources.length) {
   829:           try { console.warn('[MUMS] Supabase SDK failed to load from all sources; app will run in limited mode.'); } catch (_) {}
   830:           return;
   831:         }
   832:         const src = sources[i];
   833:         const s = document.createElement('script');
   834:         s.defer = true;
   835:         s.src = src;
   836:         s.onload = function () {
   837:           try { window.__MUMS_SUPABASE_SRC = src; } catch (_) {}
   838:         };
   839:         s.onerror = function () { loadAt(i + 1); };
   840:         document.head.appendChild(s);
   841:       }
   842: 
   843:       loadAt(0);
   844:     })();
   845:   </script>
   846:   <script src="/js/config/version.js?v=p1-516"></script>
   847:   <script src="/js/config.js?v=p1-516"></script>
   848:   <script src="/js/store.js?v=p1-516"></script>
   849:   <script src="/js/cloud_auth.js?v=p1-516"></script>
   850:   <script src="/js/cloud_users.js?v=p1-516"></script>
   851:   <script src="/js/cloud_tasks.js?v=p1-516"></script>
   852:   <script src="/js/auth.js?v=p1-516"></script>
   853:   <script src="/js/realtime.js?v=p1-516"></script>
   854:   <script src="/js/sync_status_ui.js?v=p1-516"></script>
   855:   <script src="/js/ui.js?v=p1-516"></script>
   856:   <script>try{ window.UI && UI.initAppCursor && UI.initAppCursor(); }catch(e){} </script>
   857:

     1: /* @AI_CRITICAL_GUARD v3.0: UNTOUCHABLE ZONE — MACE APPROVAL REQUIRED.
     2:    Protects: Enterprise UI/UX · Realtime Sync Logic · Core State Management ·

     6:    first submitting a RISK IMPACT REPORT to MACE and receiving explicit "CLEARED" approval.
     7:    Violations will cause regressions. When in doubt — STOP and REPORT. */

     9: 
    10: /*
    11:   CloudAuth (Supabase Auth wrapper)
    12:   ------------------------------------------------------------
    13:   This module intentionally provides backward-compatible method
    14:   names used across earlier iterations of the app:
    15:     - isEnabled() / enabled()
    16:     - signIn()    / login()
    17:     - signOut()   / logout()
    18: 
    19:   Only SAFE (public) env values are used from /api/env.
    20: */
    21: (function(){
    22:   const DBG = (window.MUMS_DEBUG || {log(){},warn(){},error(){}});
    23: 
    24:   const LS_SESSION = 'mums_supabase_session';
    25:   // In-memory fallback for browsers/environments where localStorage is blocked
    26:   // (e.g., strict privacy/tracking prevention settings).
    27:   let memSession = null;
    28:   let refreshTimer = null;
    29: 
    30: 
    31:   function _cookieSecureFlag(){
    32:     try { return (location && location.protocol === 'https:'); } catch (_) { return false; }
    33:   }
    34:   function _setCookie(name, value, days){
    35:     try {
    36:       const maxAge = (days ? (days * 86400) : 86400 * 30);
    37:       const secure = _cookieSecureFlag() ? '; Secure' : '';
    38:       document.cookie = `${name}=${encodeURIComponent(value)}; Path=/; Max-Age=${maxAge}; SameSite=Lax${secure}`;
    39:     } catch (_) {}
    40:   }
    41:   function _getCookie(name){
    42:     try {
    43:       const m = document.cookie.match(new RegExp('(?:^|; )' + name.replace(/([.$?*|{}()\[\]\\\/\+^])/g,'\\$1') + '=([^;]*)'));
    44:       return m ? decodeURIComponent(m[1]) : '';
    45:     } catch (_) { return ''; }
    46:   }
    47:   function _delCookie(name){
    48:     try {
    49:       const secure = _cookieSecureFlag() ? '; Secure' : '';
    50:       document.cookie = `${name}=; Path=/; Max-Age=0; SameSite=Lax${secure}`;
    51:     } catch (_) {}
    52:   }
    53:   function _minifySession(session){
    54:     if(!session) return null;
    55:     const u = session.user || null;
    56:     let expires_at = session.expires_at || null;
    57:     if(!expires_at && session.expires_in){
    58:       expires_at = Math.floor(Date.now()/1000) + Number(session.expires_in||0);
    59:     }
    60:     return {
    61:       access_token: session.access_token || '',
    62:       refresh_token: session.refresh_token || '',
    63:       expires_at,
    64:       user: u ? { id: u.id, email: u.email } : null
    65:     };
    66:   }
    67: 
    68: 
    69:   function env(){
    70:     try{ return (window.EnvRuntime && EnvRuntime.env) ? EnvRuntime.env() : (window.MUMS_ENV || {}); }catch(_){ return (window.MUMS_ENV || {}); }
    71:   }
    72: 
    73:   // FIX v3.9.24: 10s AbortController timeout added.
    74:   // Without this, 502/CORS from a new/throttled Supabase project hangs fetch
    75:   // indefinitely causing login button to be stuck for 1-2 minutes with no feedback.
    76:   function apiFetch(path, opts){
    77:     const e = env();
    78:     const base = String(e.SUPABASE_URL || '').replace(/\/$/, '');
    79:     const anon = String(e.SUPABASE_ANON_KEY || '');
    80:     if(!base || !anon) throw new Error('Supabase env missing (SUPABASE_URL/SUPABASE_ANON_KEY)');
    81: 
    82:     const url = base + path;
    83:     const o = Object.assign({ method:'GET', headers:{} }, (opts||{}));
    84:     o.headers = Object.assign({
    85:       'apikey': anon,
    86:       'Authorization': `Bearer ${anon}`
    87:     }, (o.headers||{}));
    88: 
    89:     // 10s timeout — prevents login hanging on 502/CORS/throttled Supabase.
    90:     const controller = new AbortController();
    91:     const timer = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), 10000);
    92:     o.signal = controller.signal;
    93:     return fetch(url, o).finally(() => clearTimeout(timer));
    94:   }
    95: 
    96:   function readSession(){
    97:     // Priority: localStorage → sessionStorage → cookie → in-memory
    98:     try{
    99:       const raw = localStorage.getItem(LS_SESSION);
   100:       if(raw){ return JSON.parse(raw); }
   101:     }catch(e){
   102:       DBG.warn('cloud_auth.readSession.localStorage_error', {e: String(e)});
   103:     }
   104:     try{
   105:       const raw = sessionStorage.getItem(LS_SESSION);
   106:       if(raw){ return JSON.parse(raw); }
   107:     }catch(e){
   108:       DBG.warn('cloud_auth.readSession.sessionStorage_error', {e: String(e)});
   109:     }
   110:     try{
   111:       const raw = _getCookie(LS_SESSION);
   112:       if(raw){ return JSON.parse(raw); }
   113:     }catch(e){
   114:       DBG.warn('cloud_auth.readSession.cookie_error', {e: String(e)});
   115:     }
   116:     if(memSession) return memSession;
   117:     return null;
   118:   }
   119: 
   120:   function writeSession(session){
   121:     // IMPORTANT: Supabase token payloads can be large. Persist a minimal, stable
   122:     // session shape to avoid cookie/localStorage bloat and reduce login/reconnect loops.
   123:     const min = _minifySession(session || null);
   124:     memSession = min;
   125:     const payload = JSON.stringify(min || null);
   126:     try{ localStorage.setItem(LS_SESSION, payload); }
   127:     catch(e){ DBG.warn('cloud_auth.writeSession.localStorage_error', {e: String(e), len: payload.length}); }
   128:     try{ sessionStorage.setItem(LS_SESSION, payload); }
   129:     catch(e){ DBG.warn('cloud_auth.writeSession.sessionStorage_error', {e: String(e), len: payload.length}); }
   130:     // Cookies are size-limited; best-effort only (minified payload).
   131:     try{ _setCookie(LS_SESSION, payload, 30); }
   132:     catch(e){ DBG.warn('cloud_auth.writeSession.cookie_error', {e: String(e), len: payload.length}); }
   133:   }
   134: 
   135:   function clearSession(){
   136:     memSession = null;
   137:     try{ localStorage.removeItem(LS_SESSION); }catch(_){ }
   138:     try{ sessionStorage.removeItem(LS_SESSION); }catch(_){ }
   139:     try{ _delCookie(LS_SESSION); }catch(_){ }
   140:   }
   141: 
   142:   function emitToken(){
   143:     try {
   144:       const t = accessToken();
   145:       if (!t) return;
   146:       window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('mums:authtoken', { detail: { token: t } }));
   147:     } catch (_) {}
   148:   }
   149: 
   150:   function clearRefreshTimer(){
   151:     try { if (refreshTimer) { clearTimeout(refreshTimer); refreshTimer = null; } } catch (_) {}
   152:   }
   153: 
   154:   async function refreshSession(){
   155:     try {
   156:       const s = readSession();
   157:       const rt = s && s.refresh_token ? String(s.refresh_token) : '';
   158:       if (!rt) return { ok:false, message:'missing_refresh_token' };
   159: 
   160:       const r = await apiFetch('/auth/v1/token?grant_type=refresh_token', {
   161:         method:'POST',
   162:         headers:{'Content-Type':'application/json'},
   163:         body: JSON.stringify({ refresh_token: rt })
   164:       });
   165: 
   166:       const bodyText = await r.text().catch(()=> '');
   167:       let data = null;
   168:       try{ data = bodyText ? JSON.parse(bodyText) : null; }catch(_){ data = null; }
   169: 
   170:       if (!r.ok || !data || !data.access_token) {
   171:         // If refresh fails, force re-login on next protected action.
   172:         return { ok:false, message:'refresh_failed', status:r.status, data };
   173:       }
   174: 
   175:       // Preserve refresh token if not returned.
   176:       if (!data.refresh_token && s && s.refresh_token) data.refresh_token = s.refresh_token;
   177: 
   178:       // Normalize expires_at if missing.
   179:       if (!data.expires_at && data.expires_in) {
   180:         data.expires_at = Math.floor(Date.now()/1000) + Number(data.expires_in);
   181:       }
   182: 
   183:       writeSession(data);
   184:       emitToken();
   185:       scheduleRefresh(data);
   186:       return { ok:true, session:data };
   187:     } catch (_) {}
   188:     return { ok:false, message:'refresh_exception' };
   189:   }
   190: 
   191:   function scheduleRefresh(session){
   192:     clearRefreshTimer();
   193:     try {
   194:       const s = session || readSession();
   195:       if (!s) return;
   196:       const expiresAt = s.expires_at ? Number(s.expires_at) : 0;
   197:       let msUntil = 0;
   198:       if (expiresAt) {
   199:         msUntil = Math.max(0, (expiresAt * 1000) - Date.now());
   200:       } else if (s.expires_in) {
   201:         msUntil = Math.max(0, Number(s.expires_in) * 1000);
   202:       }
   203:       // Refresh 60s before expiry (min 15s).
   204:       const refreshIn = Math.max(15000, msUntil - 60000);
   205:       refreshTimer = setTimeout(refreshSession, refreshIn);
   206:     } catch (_) {}
   207:   }
   208: 
   209: 
   210: 
   211: // ----------------------
   212: // JWT expiry hardening
   213: // ----------------------
   214: function _b64UrlDecodeToJson(b64url){
   215:   try{
   216:     let b64 = String(b64url || '').replace(/-/g, '+').replace(/_/g, '/');
   217:     while (b64.length % 4) b64 += '=';
   218:     const json = atob(b64);
   219:     return JSON.parse(json);
   220:   }catch(_){
   221:     return null;
   222:   }
   223: }
   224: 
   225: function decodeJwtPayload(token){
   226:   try{
   227:     const t = String(token || '');
   228:     const parts = t.split('.');
   229:     if (parts.length !== 3) return null;
   230:     return _b64UrlDecodeToJson(parts[1]);
   231:   }catch(_){
   232:     return null;
   233:   }
   234: }
   235: 
   236: function jwtExpSeconds(token){
   237:   try{
   238:     const p = decodeJwtPayload(token);
   239:     if(!p) return null;
   240:     const exp = p.exp;
   241:     // exp must be a finite number in seconds since epoch.
   242:     if (typeof exp !== 'number') return null;
   243:     if (!isFinite(exp) || exp <= 0) return null;
   244:     // Guard against accidental ms timestamps (should be ~1e9..2e9 currently).
   245:     if (exp > 10_000_000_000) return null;
   246:     return exp;
   247:   }catch(_){
   248:     return null;
   249:   }
   250: }
   251: 
   252: function isJwtExpired(token, leewaySec){
   253:   const t = String(token || '');
   254:   if(!t) return true;
   255:   const exp = jwtExpSeconds(t);
   256:   if(!exp) return true; // invalid token shape/claims
   257:   const now = Math.floor(Date.now()/1000);
   258:   const leeway = Math.max(0, Number(leewaySec || 0));
   259:   return now >= (exp - leeway);
   260: }
   261: 
   262: // Ensure the stored session is usable. If access_token is expired/invalid,
   263: // attempt a silent refresh using refresh_token (if available).
   264: async function ensureFreshSession(opts){
   265:   opts = opts || {};
   266:   const leewaySec = (opts.leewaySec !== undefined) ? Number(opts.leewaySec) : 30;
   267:   const tryRefresh = (opts.tryRefresh !== false);
   268:   const clearOnFail = (opts.clearOnFail !== false);
   269: 
   270:   const s = readSession();
   271:   if(!s || !s.access_token) return { ok:true, status:'no_session' };
   272: 
   273:   const token = String(s.access_token || '');
   274:   if(!token) return { ok:false, status:'missing_token' };
   275: 
   276:   // Exp / claim sanity
   277:   const exp = jwtExpSeconds(token);
   278:   if(!exp){
   279:     if (tryRefresh) {
   280:       const rr = await refreshSession();
   281:       if (rr && rr.ok) return { ok:true, status:'refreshed', refreshed:true };
   282:     }
   283:     if (clearOnFail) { clearRefreshTimer(); clearSession(); }
   284:     return { ok:false, status:'invalid_token' };
   285:   }
   286: 
   287:   if(isJwtExpired(token, leewaySec)){
   288:     if (tryRefresh) {
   289:       const rr = await refreshSession();
   290:       if (rr && rr.ok) return { ok:true, status:'refreshed', refreshed:true };
   291:     }
   292:     if (clearOnFail) { clearRefreshTimer(); clearSession(); }
   293:     return { ok:false, status:'expired' };
   294:   }
   295: 
   296:   return { ok:true, status:'valid' };
   297: }
   298: 
   299: // Resume guards: browsers pause timers during sleep. When the tab wakes up,
   300: // refresh an about-to-expire token immediately; otherwise force a clean relogin.
   301: let __resumeCheckAt = 0;
   302: let __resumePromise = null;
   303: const RESUME_THROTTLE_MS = 4000;
   304: 
   305: async function __runResumeCheck(trigger){
   306:   try{
   307:     const now = Date.now();
   308:     if ((now - __resumeCheckAt) < RESUME_THROTTLE_MS) return;
   309:     __resumeCheckAt = now;
   310: 
   311:     if (__resumePromise) return await __resumePromise;
   312: 
   313:     __resumePromise = (async function(){
   314:       try{
   315:         const res = await ensureFreshSession({ tryRefresh:true, clearOnFail:false, leewaySec: 60 });
   316:         if (res && res.ok) return res;
   317: 
   318:         // Hard stop: clear session to prevent 401/403 spam and notify the app.
   319:         try { clearRefreshTimer(); clearSession(); } catch(_) {}
   320:         try {
   321:           window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('mums:auth_invalid', { detail: { reason: (res && res.status) ? res.status : 'invalid', trigger: trigger || 'resume' } }));
   322:         } catch (_) {}
   323:         return res || { ok:false, status:'invalid' };
   324:       } finally {
   325:         __resumePromise = null;
   326:       }
   327:     })();
   328: 
   329:     return await __resumePromise;
   330:   }catch(_){
   331:     return { ok:false, status:'resume_exception' };
   332:   }
   333: }
   334: 
   335: function installResumeGuards(){
   336:   try{
   337:     document.addEventListener('visibilitychange', function(){
   338:       try{ if (!document.hidden) __runResumeCheck('visibility'); }catch(_){}
   339:     });
   340:   }catch(_){}
   341:   try{ window.addEventListener('focus', function(){ __runResumeCheck('focus'); }); }catch(_){}
   342:   try{ window.addEventListener('pageshow', function(){ __runResumeCheck('pageshow'); }); }catch(_){}
   343:   try{ window.addEventListener('online', function(){ __runResumeCheck('online'); }); }catch(_){}
   344: }
   345: 
   346: installResumeGuards();
   347: 
   348: function buildOAuthAuthorizeUrl(provider, opts){
   349:   const e = env();
   350:   const base = String(e.SUPABASE_URL || '').replace(/\/$/, '');
   351:   const anon = String(e.SUPABASE_ANON_KEY || '');
   352:   if(!base || !anon) return '';
   353:   const options = opts || {};
   354:   const url = new URL(base + '/auth/v1/authorize');
   355:   url.searchParams.set('provider', String(provider || '').trim());
   356:   url.searchParams.set('scopes', String(options.scopes || 'email'));
   357:   url.searchParams.set('redirect_to', String(options.redirectTo || window.location.origin));
   358:   url.searchParams.set('skip_http_redirect', 'false');
   359:   return url.toString();
   360: }
   361: 
   362: async function signInWithAzure(opts){
   363:   try{
   364:     const url = buildOAuthAuthorizeUrl('azure', opts || {});
   365:     if(!url) return { ok:false, message:'Supabase env missing (SUPABASE_URL/SUPABASE_ANON_KEY)' };
   366:     window.location.assign(url);
   367:     return { ok:true };
   368:   }catch(e){
   369:     return { ok:false, message:String(e && e.message ? e.message : e) };
   370:   }
   371: }
   372: 
   373: async function login(usernameOrEmail, password){
   374:   const e = env();
   375:   const domain = String(e.USERNAME_EMAIL_DOMAIN || 'mums.local');
   376: 
   377:   const id = String(usernameOrEmail||'').trim();
   378:   if(!id) return { ok:false, message:'Missing username/email.' };
   379: 
   380:   // Guard: never hit the password grant endpoint with an empty/undefined password.
   381:   // This prevents noisy 400s ("missing password") from accidental callers.
   382:   const pw = String(password || '').trim();
   383:   if (!pw) return { ok:false, message:'Missing password.' };
   384: 
   385:   // Accept either full email or username.
   386:   const canonicalEmail = id.includes('@') ? id : `${id}@${domain}`;
   387: 
   388:   // FIX v3.9.24: 502/abort handling + stale-session clear before retry.
   389:   // Old behaviour: 502 returned CORS error (no headers) → fetch threw → hung.
   390:   // New behaviour: catch abort/CORS → clear stored session → single retry after 2s.
   391:   async function passwordGrant(email, attempt){
   392:     attempt = attempt || 1;
   393:     let r;
   394:     try {
   395:       r = await apiFetch('/auth/v1/token?grant_type=password', {
   396:         method:'POST',
   397:         headers:{'Content-Type':'application/json'},
   398:         body: JSON.stringify({ email, password: pw })
   399:       });
   400:     } catch(fetchErr) {
   401:       // AbortError (10s timeout) or CORS/network failure
   402:       const isAbort = fetchErr && fetchErr.name === 'AbortError';
   403:       if (attempt < 2) {
   404:         // Clear any stale session that might be causing the 502 loop, then retry once
   405:         try { clearRefreshTimer(); clearSession(); } catch(_) {}
   406:         await new Promise(res => setTimeout(res, 2000));
   407:         return passwordGrant(email, 2);
   408:       }
   409:       return { ok:false, message: isAbort ? 'Login timed out. Supabase may be starting up — please try again in 30 seconds.' : 'Network error. Please check your connection and try again.', status: 0 };
   410:     }
   411: 
   412:     const bodyText = await r.text().catch(()=> '');
   413:     let data = null;
   414:     try{ data = bodyText ? JSON.parse(bodyText) : null; }catch(_){ data = null; }
   415: 
   416:     if(!r.ok){
   417:       // 502 = Supabase temporarily unavailable (new project warming up or throttled)
   418:       if (r.status === 502 || r.status === 503) {
   419:         try { clearRefreshTimer(); clearSession(); } catch(_) {}
   420:         if (attempt < 2) {
   421:           await new Promise(res => setTimeout(res, 2000));
   422:           return passwordGrant(email, 2);
   423:         }
   424:         return { ok:false, message: 'Supabase is temporarily unavailable (502). Please wait 30 seconds and try again.', status: r.status };
   425:       }
   426:       const msg = (data && (data.error_description || data.msg || data.message || data.error)) ? (data.error_description || data.msg || data.message || data.error) : bodyText;
   427:       return { ok:false, message: String(msg || 'Login failed.'), status:r.status, data };
   428:     }
   429:     // Supabase returns access_token + user
   430:     if (!data.expires_at && data.expires_in) {
   431:       data.expires_at = Math.floor(Date.now()/1000) + Number(data.expires_in);
   432:     }
   433:     return { ok:true, session:data, user:data.user || null };
   434:   }
   435: 
   436:   // First attempt: canonical email (username@domain or the email provided).
   437:   let out = await passwordGrant(canonicalEmail);
   438: 
   439:   // Legacy fallback: if the user typed a username and canonical fails, try resolving to a stored profile email.
   440:   if(!out.ok && !id.includes('@')){
   441:     const msg = String(out.message || '').toLowerCase();
   442:     const mightBeEmailMismatch = msg.includes('invalid login credentials') || msg.includes('invalid_grant') || msg.includes('invalid grant');
   443:     if(mightBeEmailMismatch){
   444:       try{
   445:         const rr = await fetch('/api/users/resolve_email?username=' + encodeURIComponent(id), { method:'GET' });
   446:         const dd = await rr.json().catch(()=> ({}));
   447:         // dd may be either the direct payload or {ok:true,data:{...}} depending on caller.
   448:         const resolved = (dd && (dd.resolved_email || (dd.data && dd.data.resolved_email))) ? (dd.resolved_email || (dd.data && dd.data.resolved_email)) : '';
   449:         if(rr.ok && resolved && String(resolved).toLowerCase() !== String(canonicalEmail).toLowerCase()){
   450:           out = await passwordGrant(resolved);
   451:         }
   452:       }catch(_){}
   453:     }
   454:   }
   455: 
   456:   if(!out.ok) return { ok:false, message: out.message || 'Login failed.' };
   457: 
   458:   writeSession(out.session);
   459:   emitToken();
   460:   scheduleRefresh(out.session);
   461:   return { ok:true, session:out.session, user:out.user || null };
   462: }
   463: 
   464: 
   465:   async function logout(){
   466:     // Best-effort: clear local session. (Server revoke is optional.)
   467:     clearRefreshTimer();
   468:     clearSession();
   469:     return { ok:true };
   470:   }
   471: 
   472:   function accessToken(){
   473:   const s = readSession();
   474:   const t = (s && s.access_token) ? String(s.access_token) : '';
   475:   if (!t) return '';
   476:   // Never expose expired/invalid JWTs to callers (prevents 401/403 spam + InvalidJWTToken crashes).
   477:   try{
   478:     if (isJwtExpired(t, 10)) return '';
   479:   }catch(_){
   480:     return '';
   481:   }
   482:   return t;
   483: }
   484: 
   485:   function getUser(){
   486:     const s = readSession();
   487:     return (s && s.user) ? s.user : null;
   488:   }
   489: 
   490:   function enabled(){
   491:     const e = env();
   492:     if(e.SUPABASE_URL && e.SUPABASE_ANON_KEY) return true;
   493:     // Cold start: EnvRuntime may not be ready yet. If a session exists, allow auth-dependent flows.
   494:     try{
   495:       const s = readSession();
   496:       if(s && (s.access_token || (s.session && s.session.access_token))){
   497:         DBG.warn('cloud_auth.enabled.env_missing_but_session_present', {hasUrl: !!e.SUPABASE_URL, hasKey: !!e.SUPABASE_ANON_KEY});
   498:         return true;
   499:       }
   500:     }catch(e){}
   501:     return false;
   502:   }
   503: 
   504:   function absorbOAuthCallbackSession(){
   505:     try{
   506:       const hash = String(window.location.hash || '').replace(/^#/, '');
   507:       if(!hash) return false;
   508:       const params = new URLSearchParams(hash);
   509: 
   510:       // OAuth provider/database-trigger failures are returned in the callback hash.
   511:       // Surface the exact description so login UI can show the real failure reason
   512:       // instead of silently looping back to the login page.
   513:       if (params.get('error')) {
   514:         const error = String(params.get('error') || 'oauth_error');
   515:         const errorDescription = String(params.get('error_description') || params.get('errorDescription') || error || 'OAuth sign-in failed.');
   516:         try {
   517:           localStorage.setItem('mums_login_flash', errorDescription);
   518:         } catch (_) {}
   519:         try {
   520:           window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('mums:oauth_error', { detail: { error, error_description: errorDescription } }));
   521:         } catch (_) {}
   522:         try {
   523:           const cleanError = window.location.pathname + window.location.search;
   524:           window.history.replaceState({}, document.title, cleanError);
   525:         } catch (_) {}
   526:         return false;
   527:       }
   528: 
   529:       if(!hash.includes('access_token=')) return false;
   530:       const access_token = String(params.get('access_token') || '');
   531:       if(!access_token) return false;
   532:       const refresh_token = String(params.get('refresh_token') || '');
   533:       const expires_in = parseInt(String(params.get('expires_in') || '0'), 10) || 0;
   534:       const expires_at = expires_in > 0 ? (Math.floor(Date.now()/1000) + expires_in) : null;
   535:       const payload = decodeJwtPayload(access_token) || {};
   536:       const user = {
   537:         id: String(payload.sub || params.get('user_id') || ''),
   538:         email: String(payload.email || params.get('email') || '')
   539:       };
   540:       const prev = readSession() || {};
   541:       const mergedUser = {
   542:         id: user.id || (prev.user && prev.user.id) || '',
   543:         email: user.email || (prev.user && prev.user.email) || ''
   544:       };
   545:       writeSession({ access_token, refresh_token: refresh_token || (prev.refresh_token || ''), expires_at, user: mergedUser });
   546:       try { localStorage.removeItem('mums_login_flash'); } catch (_) {}
   547:       emitToken();
   548:       scheduleRefresh(readSession());
   549:       try{
   550:         const clean = window.location.pathname + window.location.search;
   551:         window.history.replaceState({}, document.title, clean);
   552:       }catch(_){ }
   553: 
   554:       // Successful OAuth callback on login page should continue to app shell.
   555:       try {
   556:         const p = String(window.location.pathname || '').toLowerCase();
   557:         if (p.endsWith('/login.html') || p.endsWith('/login')) {
   558:           window.location.replace('./dashboard');
   559:         }
   560:       } catch (_) {}
   561:       return true;
   562:     }catch(_){
   563:       return false;
   564:     }
   565:   }
   566: 
   567:   // Backward-compatible wrappers
   568:   async function signIn(usernameOrEmail, password){
   569:     const out = await login(usernameOrEmail, password);
   570:     if(out && out.ok) return { ok:true, user: out.user, session: out.session };
   571:     return { ok:false, message: (out && out.message) ? out.message : 'Login failed.' };
   572:   }
   573: 
   574:   async function signOut(){
   575:     return logout();
   576:   }
   577: 
   578:   window.CloudAuth = {
   579:     // Canonical
   580:     enabled,
   581:     login,
   582:     logout,
   583:     accessToken,
   584:     loadSession: readSession,
   585:     getUser,
   586:     refreshSession,
   587:     ensureFreshSession,
   588:     signInWithAzure,
   589: 
   590:     // Compatibility
   591:     isEnabled: enabled,
   592:     signIn,
   593:     signOut
   594:   };
   595: 
   596:   try { absorbOAuthCallbackSession(); } catch (_) {}
   597: 
   598:   // If a session is already present (page reload / new tab), validate/refresh it
   599: // immediately so realtime/polling does not resume with a stale/invalid JWT.
   600: try {
   601:   const s = readSession();
   602:   if (s && s.access_token) {
   603:     // FIX v3.9.24: On 502/network error at boot, clear stale session immediately.
   604:     // Stale sessions from old Supabase project cause a refresh loop → CORS spam.
   605:     ensureFreshSession({ tryRefresh:true, clearOnFail:false, leewaySec: 60 })
   606:       .then(function(res){
   607:         try {
   608:           const t = accessToken();
   609:           if (t) emitToken();
   610:           const ss = readSession();
   611:           if (ss && ss.access_token) scheduleRefresh(ss);
   612:         } catch (_) {}
   613:         try {
   614:           if (res && !res.ok && (res.status === 'expired' || res.status === 'invalid_token' || res.status === 'refresh_failed' || res.status === 'invalid')) {
   615:             clearRefreshTimer();
   616:             clearSession();
   617:             window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('mums:auth_invalid', { detail: { reason: res.status, trigger: 'boot' } }));
   618:           }
   619:         } catch (_) {}
   620:       })
   621:       .catch(function(){
   622:         // Network error at boot (502/CORS) — clear session to stop retry loops
   623:         try { clearRefreshTimer(); clearSession(); } catch(_) {}
   624:       });
   625:   }
   626: } catch (_) {}
   627: })();

     9: 
    10: /*
    11:   Realtime (Collaboration Sync)
    12:   -------------------------------------------------------------------
    13:   - Local dev: optional WebSocket relay (ws://localhost...) for fast iteration.
    14:   - Production: cloud sync via Supabase (Vercel functions /api/sync/*).
    15: 
    16:   Data model:
    17:   - Only shared/team-interaction features are synced (announcements, schedules,
    18:     mailbox state/tables, cases, team reminders, activity logs, etc.).
    19:   - Per-user preferences (theme, world clock, quick links) are NOT synced here.
    20: 
    21:   Sync modes:
    22:   - Green  : realtime channel (Supabase Realtime) active
    23:   - Yellow : connecting / reconnecting
    24:   - Red    : offline (blocked when realtime is mandatory)
    25: */

   108:   const clientId = (function(){

   111:       let v = localStorage.getItem(k);
   112:       if (!v) {
   113:         v = 'c_' + Math.random().toString(36).slice(2) + '_' + Date.now().toString(36);
   114:         localStorage.setItem(k, v);

   122:   function dispatchStatus(mode, detail){

   126:       // can read current state even if they missed earlier mums:syncstatus events.

   139:     if (_lastBadgeState.s === state && !detail) return;
   140:     _lastBadgeState.s = state;
   141:     const mode = (state === 'connected') ? 'realtime'
   142:       : (state === 'connecting') ? 'connecting'
   143:       : (state === 'error') ? 'error'
   144:       : String(state || 'offline');
