     9: 
    10: (function(){
    11:   function encodeQuickbaseLiteral(value) {
    12:     return String(value == null ? '' : value).replace(/'/g, "\\'");
    13:   }
    14: 
    15:   function normalizeQuickbaseOperator(value) {
    16:     const key = String(value == null ? 'EX' : value).trim().toUpperCase();
    17:     const map = {
    18:       'IS EQUAL TO': 'EX',
    19:       'IS (EXACT)': 'EX',
    20:       EX: 'EX',
    21:       '=': 'EX',
    22:       'IS NOT': 'XEX',
    23:       'NOT EQUAL TO': 'XEX',
    24:       'IS NOT EQUAL TO': 'XEX',
    25:       XEX: 'XEX',
    26:       '!=': 'XEX',
    27:       '<>': 'XEX',
    28:       CONTAINS: 'CT',
    29:       CT: 'CT',
    30:       'DOES NOT CONTAIN': 'XCT',
    31:       XCT: 'XCT'
    32:     };
    33:     return map[key] || key || 'EX';
    34:   }
    35: 
    36:   function parseQuickbaseSettings(raw) {
    37:     if (!raw) return {};
    38:     if (typeof raw === 'object') return raw;
    39:     try {
    40:       const parsed = JSON.parse(String(raw));
    41:       return parsed && typeof parsed === 'object' ? parsed : {};
    42:     } catch (_) {
    43:       return {};
    44:     }
    45:   }
    46: 
    47: 
    48:   function hasOwnKeys(obj) {
    49:     return !!obj && typeof obj === 'object' && !Array.isArray(obj) && Object.keys(obj).length > 0;
    50:   }
    51: 
    52:   function buildQuickbaseWhere(filters, matchMode) {
    53:     if (!Array.isArray(filters) || !filters.length) return '';
    54: 
    55:     // INCLUSION operators: same-field values are OR'd together.
    56:     //   e.g. Type=A + Type=B → (Type=A OR Type=B) [record matches any value]
    57:     // EXCLUSION operators: each condition is kept as its own AND clause.
    58:     //   e.g. Status!=Resolved + Status!=Bug → Status!=Resolved AND Status!=Bug
    59:     //   Grouping XEX with OR is logically always-true (a record is always "not"
    60:     //   at least one of N values) so exclusions must NEVER be OR-grouped.
    61:     var INCLUSION_OPS = { EX: 1, CT: 1, SW: 1, BF: 1, AF: 1, LT: 1, LTE: 1, GT: 1, GTE: 1, IR: 1, TV: 1 };
    62:     var isAny = String(matchMode || '').trim().toUpperCase() === 'ANY';
    63: 
    64:     // Group INCLUSION filters by fieldId
    65:     var inclusionByField = new Map();
    66:     // Keep EXCLUSION filters flat
    67:     var exclusionClauses = [];
    68: 
    69:     filters
    70:       .filter(function(f){ return f && typeof f === 'object'; })
    71:       .forEach(function(f){
    72:         var fid = String(f.fieldId != null ? f.fieldId : (f.field_id != null ? f.field_id : (f.fid != null ? f.fid : (f.id != null ? f.id : '')))).trim();
    73:         var value = String(f.value != null ? f.value : '').trim();
    74:         var operator = normalizeQuickbaseOperator(f.operator);
    75:         if (!fid || !value) return;
    76:         var clause = '{' + fid + '.' + operator + '.\'' + encodeQuickbaseLiteral(value) + '\'}';
    77:         if (INCLUSION_OPS[operator]) {
    78:           if (!inclusionByField.has(fid)) inclusionByField.set(fid, []);
    79:           inclusionByField.get(fid).push(clause);
    80:         } else {
    81:           exclusionClauses.push(clause);
    82:         }
    83:       });
    84: 
    85:     // Build per-field inclusion clauses (OR within same field)
    86:     var inclusionFieldClauses = [];
    87:     inclusionByField.forEach(function(clauses) {
    88:       if (!clauses.length) return;
    89:       inclusionFieldClauses.push(clauses.length === 1 ? clauses[0] : '(' + clauses.join(' OR ') + ')');
    90:     });
    91: 
    92:     var allParts = [];
    93: 
    94:     if (isAny) {
    95:       // ANY mode: all inclusion field-groups joined with OR
    96:       if (inclusionFieldClauses.length > 0) {
    97:         allParts.push(inclusionFieldClauses.length === 1
    98:           ? inclusionFieldClauses[0]
    99:           : '(' + inclusionFieldClauses.join(' OR ') + ')');
   100:       }
   101:     } else {
   102:       // ALL mode: each inclusion field-group AND'd with the next
   103:       inclusionFieldClauses.forEach(function(c) { allParts.push(c); });
   104:     }
   105: 
   106:     // Exclusion clauses always AND'd individually (regardless of matchMode)
   107:     exclusionClauses.forEach(function(c) { allParts.push(c); });
   108: 
   109:     if (!allParts.length) return '';
   110:     return allParts.join(' AND ');
   111:   }
   112: 
   113:   function appendParam(params, key, value) {
   114:     const clean = String(value == null ? '' : value).trim();
   115:     if (!clean) return;
   116:     params.set(key, clean);
   117:   }
   118: 
   119:   function normalizeMonitoringLimit(rawLimit) {
   120:     const parsed = Number(rawLimit);
   121:     if (!Number.isFinite(parsed) || parsed <= 0) return 100;
   122:     return Math.min(100, Math.floor(parsed));
   123:   }
   124: 
   125:   function getToken() {
   126:     try {
   127:       if (window.CloudAuth && typeof CloudAuth.accessToken === 'function') {
   128:         const t = CloudAuth.accessToken();
   129:         if (t) return String(t);
   130:       }
   131:     } catch (_) {}
   132:     try {
   133:       if (window.Store && typeof Store.getSession === 'function') {
   134:         const s = Store.getSession();
   135:         const t = s && (s.access_token || (s.session && s.session.access_token) || (s.data && s.data.session && s.data.session.access_token));
   136:         if (t) return String(t);
   137:       }
   138:     } catch (_) {}
   139:     try {
   140:       const raw = window.localStorage && window.localStorage.getItem('mums_supabase_session');
   141:       if (raw) {
   142:         const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
   143:         const t = parsed && (parsed.access_token || (parsed.session && parsed.session.access_token));
   144:         if (t) return String(t);
   145:       }
   146:     } catch (_) {}
   147:     return '';
   148:   }
   149: 
   150: 
   151:   function flattenFidFields(fields) {
   152:     const src = fields && typeof fields === 'object' ? fields : {};
   153:     const mapped = {};
   154:     Object.keys(src).forEach(function(fid){
   155:       if (!fid) return;
   156:       const raw = src[fid];
   157:       const value = (raw && typeof raw === 'object' && Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(raw, 'value'))
   158:         ? raw.value
   159:         : raw;
   160:       mapped[String(fid)] = value == null ? '' : value;
   161:     });
   162:     return mapped;
   163:   }
   164: 
   165:   function toSafePayload(payload) {
   166:     const src = payload && typeof payload === 'object' ? payload : {};
   167:     const rows = Array.isArray(src.records)
   168:       ? src.records
   169:       : (Array.isArray(src.rows) ? src.rows : []);
   170:     const columns = Array.isArray(src.columns) ? src.columns : [];
   171:     const normalizedRows = rows.map(function(r){
   172:       const normalizedFields = flattenFidFields(r && r.fields);
   173:       const fallbackRecordId = normalizedFields['3'];
   174:       const qbRecordId = String((r && r.qbRecordId) || fallbackRecordId || 'N/A');
   175:       const fields = {};
   176:       Object.keys(normalizedFields).forEach(function(fid){
   177:         fields[fid] = { value: normalizedFields[fid] == null ? '' : normalizedFields[fid] };
   178:       });
   179:       return {
   180:         qbRecordId: qbRecordId,
   181:         fields: fields
   182:       };
   183:     });
   184: 
   185:     return {
   186:       ok: !!src.ok,
   187:       warning: String(src.warning || ''),
   188:       columns: columns.map(function(c){
   189:         return {
   190:           id: String((c && c.id) || ''),
   191:           label: String((c && c.label) || '')
   192:         };
   193:       }).filter(function(c){ return !!c.id; }),
   194:       rows: normalizedRows,
   195:       records: normalizedRows,
   196:       allAvailableFields: Array.isArray(src.allAvailableFields) ? src.allAvailableFields : [],
   197:       settings: (src.settings && typeof src.settings === 'object') ? src.settings : {}
   198:     };
   199:   }
   200: 
   201:   async function fetchMonitoringData(overrideParams) {
   202:     const token = getToken();
   203:     if (!token) {
   204:       throw new Error('Quickbase auth token missing. Please login again.');
   205:     }
   206: 
   207:     // Get user profile for QID settings
   208:     let qid = '';
   209:     let tableId = '';
   210:     let realm = '';
   211:     let profileQuickbaseConfig = null;
   212: 
   213:     if (overrideParams && typeof overrideParams === 'object') {
   214:       qid = String(overrideParams.qid || '').trim();
   215:       tableId = String(overrideParams.tableId || '').trim();
   216:       realm = String(overrideParams.realm || '').trim();
   217:     }
   218: 
   219:     // Fallback to stored profile if not provided
   220:     if (!qid || !tableId || !realm) {
   221:       try {
   222:         const me = (window.Auth && Auth.getUser) ? Auth.getUser() : null;
   223:         if (me && window.Store && Store.getProfile) {
   224:           const profile = Store.getProfile(me.id);
   225:           if (profile) {
   226:             profileQuickbaseConfig = parseQuickbaseSettings(profile.quickbase_settings);
   227:             if (!profileQuickbaseConfig || !Object.keys(profileQuickbaseConfig).length) {
   228:               profileQuickbaseConfig = parseQuickbaseSettings(profile.quickbase_config);
   229:             }
   230:             qid = qid || String((profileQuickbaseConfig && (profileQuickbaseConfig.qid || profileQuickbaseConfig.qb_qid)) || profile.qb_qid || profile.quickbase_qid || '').trim();
   231:             tableId = tableId || String((profileQuickbaseConfig && (profileQuickbaseConfig.tableId || profileQuickbaseConfig.qb_table_id)) || profile.qb_table_id || profile.quickbase_table_id || '').trim();
   232:             realm = realm || String((profileQuickbaseConfig && (profileQuickbaseConfig.realm || profileQuickbaseConfig.qb_realm)) || profile.qb_realm || profile.quickbase_realm || '').trim();
   233:           }
   234:         }
   235:       } catch (_) {}
   236:     }
   237: 
   238: 
   239:     const normalizedSettings = parseQuickbaseSettings(profileQuickbaseConfig);
   240:     const normalizedSearch = String((overrideParams && overrideParams.search) || '').trim();
   241:     const hasCustomFilters = Array.isArray(overrideParams && overrideParams.customFilters) && (overrideParams.customFilters || []).length > 0;
   242:     const shouldUseDefaultReport = !hasOwnKeys(normalizedSettings) && !normalizedSearch && !hasCustomFilters;
   243: 
   244:     // Validate required params
   245:     if (!qid || !tableId || !realm) {
   246:       const missingErr = new Error('Quickbase settings not configured. Please set your QID, Table ID, and Realm in My Quickbase Settings.');
   247:       missingErr.code = 'quickbase_credentials_missing';
   248:       throw missingErr;
   249:     }
   250: 
   251:     const headers = {
   252:       'content-type': 'application/json',
   253:       authorization: 'Bearer ' + token
   254:     };
   255: 
   256:     // Construct URL with query parameters
   257:     const queryParams = new URLSearchParams({
   258:       qid: qid,
   259:       tableId: tableId,
   260:       realm: realm
   261:     });
   262:     // Bypass global QB config — use profile.qb_token on server
   263:     if (overrideParams && overrideParams.bypassGlobal) {
   264:       queryParams.set('bypassGlobal', 'true');
   265:     }
   266: 
   267:     const extraWhere = shouldUseDefaultReport ? '' : buildQuickbaseWhere(overrideParams && overrideParams.customFilters, overrideParams && overrideParams.filterMatch);
   268:     appendParam(queryParams, 'where', (overrideParams && overrideParams.where) || extraWhere);
   269:     appendParam(queryParams, 'search', normalizedSearch);
   270:     appendParam(queryParams, 'searchFields', Array.isArray(overrideParams && overrideParams.searchFields)
   271:       ? (overrideParams.searchFields || []).map(function(v){ return String(v || '').trim(); }).filter(Boolean).join(',')
   272:       : '');
   273:     appendParam(queryParams, 'limit', normalizeMonitoringLimit(overrideParams && overrideParams.limit));
   274: 
   275:     const candidates = [
   276:       '/api/quickbase/monitoring?' + queryParams.toString(),
   277:       '/functions/quickbase/monitoring?' + queryParams.toString()
   278:     ];
   279: 
   280:     let lastErr = null;
   281: 
   282:     for (const url of candidates) {
   283:       try {
   284:         const res = await fetch(url, { method: 'GET', headers, cache: 'no-store' });
   285:         const data = await res.json().catch(function(){ return {}; });
   286: 
   287:         if (!res.ok) {
   288:           const message = data && data.message ? String(data.message) : ('Endpoint ' + url + ' failed with ' + res.status);
   289:           lastErr = new Error(message);
   290:           continue;
   291:         }
   292: 
   293:         const safePayload = toSafePayload(data);
   294: 
   295:         if (safePayload.warning === 'quickbase_credentials_missing') {
   296:           const missingCredsErr = new Error('Missing Quickbase Credentials: Token or Realm not found. Please verify your Profile Settings.');
   297:           missingCredsErr.code = 'quickbase_credentials_missing';
   298:           throw missingCredsErr;
   299:         }
   300: 
   301:         try {
   302:           console.info('[Enterprise DB] Quickbase Payload (QID: ' + qid + '):', safePayload.rows);
   303:         } catch (_) {}
   304: 
   305:         return safePayload;
   306:       } catch (e) {
   307:         lastErr = e;
   308:       }
   309:     }
   310: 
   311:     throw lastErr || new Error('Quickbase endpoint unreachable');
   312:   }
   313: 
   314:   window.QuickbaseAdapter = {
   315:     fetchMonitoringData: fetchMonitoringData
   316:   };
   317: })();

   265: /* =========================================================
   266:    GLOBAL PROPORTIONAL LAYOUT SCALING (v4.2 layout-scale)
   267:    Keep the app shell stable when the viewport shrinks by scaling
   268:    the entire UI (like browser zoom) instead of reflowing columns.
   269:    Scale factor is controlled by CSS variable --app-scale (set in
   270:    public/js/env_runtime.js so it applies to login + app shells).
   271:    ========================================================= */
   272: 
   273: html{
   274:   --app-scale: 1;
   275:   overflow-x: hidden;
   276: }

   278: body{
   279:   transform: scale(var(--app-scale));
   280:   transform-origin: top left;
   281:   width: calc(100% / var(--app-scale));
   282:   min-height: calc(100vh / var(--app-scale));
   283:   overflow-x: hidden;
   284: }

     1: /* @AI_CRITICAL_GUARD v3.0: UNTOUCHABLE ZONE — MACE APPROVAL REQUIRED.
     2:    Protects: Enterprise UI/UX · Realtime Sync Logic · Core State Management ·
     3:    Database/API Adapters · Tab Isolation · Virtual Column State ·
     4:    QuickBase Settings Persistence · Auth Flow.
     5:    DO NOT modify any existing logic, layout, or structure in this file without
     6:    first submitting a RISK IMPACT REPORT to MACE and receiving explicit "CLEARED" approval.
     7:    Violations will cause regressions. When in doubt — STOP and REPORT. */
     8: 
     9: 
    10: /*
    11: ===============================================================================
    12: SYSTEM PROMPT INSTRUCTION / AI CONTRACT — STRICT DESIGN TOKEN ARCHITECTURE
    13: -------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    14: 1) CORE LAYOUT CLASSES (.card, .btn, .sidebar, grids, tables, modals, etc.)
    15:    MUST contain structure/spacing/typography only and MUST consume semantic tokens.
    16: 2) THEME BLOCKS (body[data-theme="..."]) are aesthetic overrides only.
    17:    NEVER place structural layout rules inside theme blocks.
    18: 3) NEVER hardcode colors/shadows/borders in core components.
    19:    Always use semantic variables from :root token registry.
    20: 4) Adding/editing/removing a theme must NOT break application layout.
    21: ===============================================================================
    22: */

   284: /* =========================================================
   285:    GLOBAL PROPORTIONAL LAYOUT SCALING (v4.2 layout-scale)
   286:    Keep the app shell stable when the viewport shrinks by scaling
   287:    the entire UI (like browser zoom) instead of reflowing columns.
   288:    Scale factor is controlled by CSS variable --app-scale (set in
   289:    public/js/env_runtime.js so it applies to login + app shells).
   290:    ========================================================= */

   292: html{
   293:   --app-scale: 1;
   294:   overflow: hidden;
   295:   height: 100%;
   296: }

   299:   transform: scale(var(--app-scale));
   300:   transform-origin: top left;
   301:   width: calc(100% / var(--app-scale));
   302:   height: calc(100vh / var(--app-scale));
   303:   max-height: calc(100vh / var(--app-scale));
   304:   overflow: hidden;
   305: }

  2383: /* ── Sidebar nav: layout + overflow fix ───────────────────────────────────── */
  2384: /* .side uses flex-column so the nav gets all remaining height and scrolls    */
  2385: /* internally — the parent must NOT clip Y axis or nav items get cut off.     */
  2386: .side{
  2387:   position:relative;
  2388:   overflow:hidden;          /* clip X only; flex children manage Y */
  2389:   display:flex;
  2390:   flex-direction:column;
  2391: }

  2393: /* Ensure sidebar navigation is always visible/clickable when expanded */
  2394: body:not(.sidebar-collapsed) .side #nav,

  2400: }

  2402: body:not(.sidebar-collapsed) .side .usercard{position:relative; z-index:3;}

  2406:   flex:1 1 auto;

  4662: 
  4663: /* ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  4664:    ⚠️  AI GUARDRAIL — CRITICAL LAYOUT RULE FOR ALL THEMES
  4665:    ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  4666:    RULE: Themes MUST NOT override structural layout properties.
  4667:    Themes are ONLY allowed to change:
  4668:      ✅ colors, backgrounds, gradients
  4669:      ✅ border colors and styles
  4670:      ✅ box-shadows
  4671:      ✅ font-family, font-weight, letter-spacing, text-shadow
  4672:      ✅ border-radius (decorative only)
  4673:      ✅ opacity, filter (decorative only)
  4674:      ✅ CSS custom property values (--surface-1, --text, etc.)
  4675: 
  4676:    Themes MUST NEVER override:
  4677:      ❌ display, grid-template-*, grid-column, grid-row
  4678:      ❌ position, top, right, bottom, left, z-index
  4679:      ❌ width, height, min-height, max-height
  4680:      ❌ overflow (any variant)
  4681:      ❌ flex, flex-direction, flex-grow, flex-shrink, flex-basis
  4682:      ❌ padding (shorthand) — use padding-top/right/left only,
  4683:         NEVER padding-bottom on .app (it controls dock clearance)
  4684:      ❌ margin (shorthand that resets any axis)
  4685:      ❌ gap (on .app — affects the main 3-column grid)
  4686:      ❌ align-self, justify-self on grid children (.side/.main/.right)
  4687: 
  4688:    WHY: The bottom dock (.quicklinks-bar) and the online-users-bar
  4689:    are position:fixed with heights stored in CSS variables:
  4690:      --mums-dock-h: 86px
  4691:      --mums-onlinebar-h: 44px
  4692:    The .app container MUST have padding-bottom equal to their sum
  4693:    so sidebar/content is never buried under them.
  4694:    Shorthand "padding:Xpx" resets padding-bottom to X, defeating
  4695:    the base rule "padding-bottom: calc(var(--mums-dock-h) + ...)"
  4696:    and causing all column content to be clipped by the fixed bars.
  4697: 
  4698:    CORRECT pattern for any theme that needs custom .app spacing:
  4699:      body[data-theme="your_theme"] .app {
  4700:        gap: 1px;                    ✅ OK — just changes gap
  4701:        padding: 0;                ❌ WRONG — resets padding-bottom
  4702:        padding-top: 0;            ✅ CORRECT way to set top only
  4703:        padding-left: 0;           ✅ CORRECT
  4704:        padding-right: 0;          ✅ CORRECT
  4705:        /* padding-bottom: NEVER TOUCH — set by base layout */
  4706:      }
  4707:    ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */
  4708: 
  4709: /* ── APP GRID ── */
  4710: body[data-theme="aurora_midnight"] .app {
  4711:   gap: 1px;
  4712:   padding-top: 0;
  4713:   padding-left: 0;
  4714:   padding-right: 0;
  4715: }
  4716:

  5228: 
  5229: /* ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  5230:    SIDEBAR SCROLL FIX v3 — definitive
  5231:    ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  5232: 
  5233:    Root cause (confirmed):
  5234:    Setting height:100% on a grid item that already uses the
  5235:    default align-self:stretch causes Chrome to resolve it as
  5236:    100% of the GRID CONTAINER height — not the track height.
  5237:    This makes .side taller than its track, so .nav never
  5238:    overflows it and overflow-y:auto never activates.
  5239: 
  5240:    Correct technique:
  5241:    Do NOT set height:100% on .side. Instead, set overflow:hidden.
  5242:    overflow:hidden on a grid item creates a new BFC and PREVENTS
  5243:    the flex container from growing past its grid track boundary.
  5244:    Now .side's intrinsic height = the track height (definite).
  5245:    flex:1 1 0px on .nav then correctly fills the remaining
  5246:    bounded space and overflow-y:auto activates when content
  5247:    exceeds that space.
  5248: 
  5249:    Specificity note:
  5250:    Line ~2318 has: body:not(.sidebar-collapsed) .side .nav
  5251:    { display:block } at specificity 0-3-1.
  5252:    We must use an equal or higher specificity selector below,
  5253:    placed later in the file, to override it.
  5254:    ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */
  5255: 
  5256: /* 1 ── Sidebar shell: flex column, bounded by overflow:hidden
  5257:         DO NOT add height:100% — grid stretch + overflow:hidden
  5258:         is what actually constrains the height. */
  5259: aside.side,
  5260: .side.card.pad {
  5261:   display: flex !important;
  5262:   flex-direction: column !important;
  5263:   overflow: hidden !important;
  5264:   min-height: 0 !important;
  5265: }
  5266: 
  5267: /* 2 ── Nav: fills remaining space and scrolls.
  5268:         Selector specificity 0-3-1 beats the display:block rule
  5269:         at line ~2318. !important ensures it sticks. */
  5270: body:not(.sidebar-collapsed) .side #nav,
  5271: body:not(.sidebar-collapsed) .side .nav {
  5272:   display: flex !important;
  5273:   flex-direction: column !important;
  5274:   flex: 1 1 0px !important;
  5275:   min-height: 0 !important;
  5276:   overflow-y: auto !important;
  5277:   overflow-x: hidden !important;
  5278:   padding-right: 3px !important;
  5279: }
  5280: /* Low-specificity fallback for non-collapsed state without body class */
  5281: .side #nav,
  5282: .side .nav {
  5283:   flex: 1 1 0px !important;
  5284:   min-height: 0 !important;
  5285:   overflow-y: auto !important;
  5286:   overflow-x: hidden !important;
  5287: }
  5288: 
  5289: /* 3 ── Fixed-size siblings — never grow, never push nav out */
  5290: .side .usercard {
  5291:   flex: 0 0 auto !important;
  5292: }
  5293: .side .side-logs {
  5294:   flex: 0 0 auto !important;
  5295:   margin-top: auto !important;
  5296: }
  5297: 
  5298: /* 4 ── Thin scrollbar — appears only when nav overflows */
  5299: .side #nav::-webkit-scrollbar,
  5300: .side .nav::-webkit-scrollbar { width: 3px; }
  5301: .side #nav::-webkit-scrollbar-track,
  5302: .side .nav::-webkit-scrollbar-track { background: transparent; }
  5303: .side #nav::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb,
  5304: .side .nav::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb {
  5305:   background: rgba(255,255,255,.15);
  5306:   border-radius: 999px;
  5307: }
  5308: .side #nav::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb:hover,
  5309: .side .nav::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb:hover {
  5310:   background: rgba(255,255,255,.30);
  5311: }
  5312: 
  5313: /* 5 ── Collapsed sidebar: icon-only, no scroll */
  5314: body.sidebar-collapsed aside.side,
  5315: body.sidebar-collapsed .side.card.pad {
  5316:   overflow: hidden !important;
  5317: }
  5318: 
  5319: 
  5320: /* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  5321:    MUMS BUILD 2 — SIDEBAR NAV ALIGNMENT FIX
  5322:    Overrides legacy overflow:hidden rules that were clipping nav items.
  5323:    ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */
  5324: 
  5325: /* 1. Side panel: flex column so nav fills remaining height, scrolls inside */
  5326: aside.side.card.pad,
  5327: aside.side {
  5328:   display: flex !important;
  5329:   flex-direction: column !important;
  5330:   overflow: hidden !important;   /* clip X; flex children manage Y */
  5331: }
  5332: 
  5333: /* 2. Nav: takes all available height, scrolls vertically */
  5334: aside.side .nav,
  5335: aside.side #nav {
  5336:   flex: 1 1 auto !important;
  5337:   min-height: 0 !important;
  5338:   overflow-y: auto !important;
  5339:   overflow-x: hidden !important;
  5340:   width: 100% !important;
  5341:   box-sizing: border-box !important;
  5342: }
  5343: 
  5344: /* 3. Nav group wrapper: never clip children (was cutting off MEMBERS etc.) */
  5345: .nav-group {
  5346:   overflow: visible !important;
  5347:   width: 100% !important;
  5348:   box-sizing: border-box !important;
  5349: }
  5350: 
  5351: /* 4. All nav items & group heads: full width, never overflow parent */
  5352: .nav .nav-item,
  5353: .nav .nav-group-head {
  5354:   width: 100% !important;
  5355:   max-width: 100% !important;
  5356:   box-sizing: border-box !important;
  5357:   min-width: 0 !important;
  5358:   overflow: hidden !important;  /* clip text only, not layout */
  5359: }
  5360: 
  5361: /* 5. Nav label text: clip with ellipsis, never cause layout overflow */
  5362: .nav .nav-label {
  5363:   flex: 1 1 auto !important;
  5364:   min-width: 0 !important;
  5365:   overflow: hidden !important;
  5366:   text-overflow: ellipsis !important;
  5367:   white-space: nowrap !important;
  5368: }
  5369: 
  5370: /* 6. Icon: fixed width so labels always line up */
  5371: .nav .nav-ico {
  5372:   flex: 0 0 18px !important;
  5373:   width: 18px !important;
  5374:   height: 18px !important;
  5375: }
  5376: 
  5377: /* 7. Sub-items (inside nav-group-kids): indented but still full-width */
  5378: .nav-group-kids {
  5379:   overflow: visible !important;
  5380:   padding: 2px 0 8px 0 !important;
  5381:   display: flex;
  5382:   flex-direction: column;
  5383:   gap: 1px;
  5384: }
  5385: .nav .nav-item.nav-subitem {
  5386:   width: 100% !important;
  5387:   box-sizing: border-box !important;
  5388: }
  5389: 
  5390: /* 8. Side-logs pushed to bottom of flex column */
  5391: .side .side-logs {
  5392:   flex-shrink: 0 !important;
  5393:   margin-top: auto !important;
  5394: }
  5395: .side .usercard {
  5396:   flex-shrink: 0 !important;
  5397: }

  9742: 
  9743: /* ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  9744:    BOTTOM BAR SPACER — v4 PERMANENT FIX (ResizeObserver-driven)
  9745:    ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  9746:    AUTHORITATIVE DRIVER: app.js → _initBarObservers() + _commitBarHeights()
  9747:    Uses ResizeObserver to measure the ACTUAL pixel height of each bar in
  9748:    real-time and writes to <html> inline style (highest specificity):
  9749:      document.documentElement.style.setProperty('--mums-dock-h',      actualQlHeight + 'px')
  9750:      document.documentElement.style.setProperty('--mums-onlinebar-h', actualOnlineHeight + 'px')
  9751: 
  9752:    WHY THIS IS THE PERMANENT FIX:
  9753:    • Previous bug: hardcoded 86px / 44px were SMALLER than actual rendered heights
  9754:      (bars include padding + border → real height was ~57px online, ~86px ql).
  9755:      Grid row 3 spacer was too short → sidebars/main bled under the fixed bars.
  9756:    • ResizeObserver fires whenever bar size changes (content, theme, window resize).
  9757:    • Inline style on <html> cannot be beaten by any CSS :root or theme override.
  9758: 
  9759:    CSS RULES BELOW: animation/visibility only — NO layout values.
  9760:    Do NOT add --mums-dock-h or --mums-onlinebar-h overrides here.
  9761:    ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */
  9762: 
  9763: /* Smooth grid resize when bars toggle — transition on .app row changes */
  9764: .app {
  9765:   transition: grid-template-rows .28s cubic-bezier(.4,0,.2,1);
  9766: }
  9767: 
  9768: /* Online bar bottom anchor is set by JS inline style.
  9769:    CSS fallback only (fires before JS runs): sit at bottom:0 */
  9770: .online-users-bar {
  9771:   transition: bottom .28s cubic-bezier(.4,0,.2,1),
  9772:               transform .28s cubic-bezier(.4,0,.2,1),
  9773:               opacity .22s ease;
  9774: }
  9775: 
  9776: 
  9777: /* ── PERMANENT FIX: Bottom bars must not overlap left/right sidebars ──────────
  9778:    ROOT CAUSE: .online-users-bar and .quicklinks-bar use left:0;right:0 (full viewport width).
  9779:    This causes them to visually overlap the left sidebar (244px) and right sidebar (296px).
  9780:    FIX: Introduce CSS variables for sidebar widths, driven by body classes.
  9781:    These vars are updated automatically when sidebars collapse/expand/hide.
  9782:    ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
  9783: 
  9784: :root {
  9785:   --sidebar-left-w: 245px;  /* 244px left sidebar + 1px grid gap */
  9786:   --sidebar-right-w: 297px; /* 296px right sidebar + 1px grid gap */
  9787: }
  9788: 
  9789: /* Sidebar collapsed: left becomes 72px + 1px gap */
  9790: body.sidebar-collapsed {
  9791:   --sidebar-left-w: 73px;
  9792: }
  9793: 
  9794: /* Rightbar collapsed/hidden: right becomes 0 */
  9795: body.rightbar-collapsed {
  9796:   --sidebar-right-w: 0px;
  9797: }
  9798: 
  9799: /* Apply offset to both fixed bottom bars — FULL WIDTH, edge to edge */
  9800: .online-users-bar,
  9801: .quicklinks-bar {
  9802:   left: 0 !important;
  9803:   right: 0 !important;
  9804:   transition:
  9805:     left .25s cubic-bezier(.4,0,.2,1),
  9806:     right .25s cubic-bezier(.4,0,.2,1),
  9807:     bottom .28s cubic-bezier(.4,0,.2,1),
  9808:     transform .28s cubic-bezier(.4,0,.2,1),
  9809:     opacity .22s ease;
  9810: }
  9811: 
  9812: /* Clocks and quicklinks inner are positioned absolutely inside the bar.
  9813:    Their left/right references are already relative to the bar — no extra offset needed. */
  9814: 
  9815: /* ── Responsive: on narrow screens sidebars collapse, bars go full width ── */
  9816: @media (max-width: 768px) {
  9817:   :root {
  9818:     --sidebar-left-w: 0px;
  9819:     --sidebar-right-w: 0px;
  9820:   }
  9821: }

=== FILE: css/styles.css ===
     1: /* @AI_CRITICAL_GUARD: UNTOUCHABLE ZONE. Do not modify existing UI/UX, layouts, or core logic in this file without explicitly asking MACE for clearance. If changes are required here, STOP and provide a RISK IMPACT REPORT first. */
     2: /*
     3: ===============================================================================
     4: SYSTEM PROMPT INSTRUCTION / AI CONTRACT — STRICT DESIGN TOKEN ARCHITECTURE
     5: -------------------------------------------------------------------------------
     6: 1) CORE LAYOUT CLASSES (.card, .btn, .sidebar, grids, tables, modals, etc.)
     7:    MUST contain structure/spacing/typography only and MUST consume semantic tokens.
     8: 2) THEME BLOCKS (body[data-theme="..."]) are aesthetic overrides only.
     9:    NEVER place structural layout rules inside theme blocks.
    10: 3) NEVER hardcode colors/shadows/borders in core components.
    11:    Always use semantic variables from :root token registry.
    12: 4) Adding/editing/removing a theme must NOT break application layout.
    13: ===============================================================================

   283:   overflow-x: hidden;

     1: /* @AI_CRITICAL_GUARD v3.0: UNTOUCHABLE ZONE — MACE APPROVAL REQUIRED.

     4:    QuickBase Settings Persistence · Auth Flow.
     5:    DO NOT modify any existing logic, layout, or structure in this file without

     7:    Violations will cause regressions. When in doubt — STOP and REPORT. */

    10: /*
    11: ===============================================================================
    12: SYSTEM PROMPT INSTRUCTION / AI CONTRACT — STRICT DESIGN TOKEN ARCHITECTURE
    13: -------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    14: 1) CORE LAYOUT CLASSES (.card, .btn, .sidebar, grids, tables, modals, etc.)
    15:    MUST contain structure/spacing/typography only and MUST consume semantic tokens.
    16: 2) THEME BLOCKS (body[data-theme="..."]) are aesthetic overrides only.
    17:    NEVER place structural layout rules inside theme blocks.
    18: 3) NEVER hardcode colors/shadows/borders in core components.
    19:    Always use semantic variables from :root token registry.
    20: 4) Adding/editing/removing a theme must NOT break application layout.
    21: ===============================================================================
    22: */

  4662: 
  4663: /* ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  4664:    ⚠️  AI GUARDRAIL — CRITICAL LAYOUT RULE FOR ALL THEMES
  4665:    ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  4666:    RULE: Themes MUST NOT override structural layout properties.
  4667:    Themes are ONLY allowed to change:
  4668:      ✅ colors, backgrounds, gradients
  4669:      ✅ border colors and styles
  4670:      ✅ box-shadows
  4671:      ✅ font-family, font-weight, letter-spacing, text-shadow
  4672:      ✅ border-radius (decorative only)
  4673:      ✅ opacity, filter (decorative only)
  4674:      ✅ CSS custom property values (--surface-1, --text, etc.)
  4675: 
  4676:    Themes MUST NEVER override:
  4677:      ❌ display, grid-template-*, grid-column, grid-row
  4678:      ❌ position, top, right, bottom, left, z-index
  4679:      ❌ width, height, min-height, max-height
  4680:      ❌ overflow (any variant)
  4681:      ❌ flex, flex-direction, flex-grow, flex-shrink, flex-basis
  4682:      ❌ padding (shorthand) — use padding-top/right/left only,
  4683:         NEVER padding-bottom on .app (it controls dock clearance)
  4684:      ❌ margin (shorthand that resets any axis)
  4685:      ❌ gap (on .app — affects the main 3-column grid)
  4686:      ❌ align-self, justify-self on grid children (.side/.main/.right)
  4687: 
  4688:    WHY: The bottom dock (.quicklinks-bar) and the online-users-bar
  4689:    are position:fixed with heights stored in CSS variables:
  4690:      --mums-dock-h: 86px
  4691:      --mums-onlinebar-h: 44px
  4692:    The .app container MUST have padding-bottom equal to their sum
  4693:    so sidebar/content is never buried under them.
  4694:    Shorthand "padding:Xpx" resets padding-bottom to X, defeating
  4695:    the base rule "padding-bottom: calc(var(--mums-dock-h) + ...)"
  4696:    and causing all column content to be clipped by the fixed bars.
  4697: 
  4698:    CORRECT pattern for any theme that needs custom .app spacing:
  4699:      body[data-theme="your_theme"] .app {
  4700:        gap: 1px;                    ✅ OK — just changes gap
  4701:        padding: 0;                ❌ WRONG — resets padding-bottom
  4702:        padding-top: 0;            ✅ CORRECT way to set top only
  4703:        padding-left: 0;           ✅ CORRECT
  4704:        padding-right: 0;          ✅ CORRECT
  4705:        /* padding-bottom: NEVER TOUCH — set by base layout */
  4706:      }
  4707:    ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */
  4708: 
  4709: /* ── APP GRID ── */
  4710: body[data-theme="aurora_midnight"] .app {
  4711:   gap: 1px;
  4712:   padding-top: 0;
  4713:   padding-left: 0;
  4714:   padding-right: 0;
  4715: }

  5224: body[data-theme="mono"]{
  5225:   --surface-bg:#0b0c10;--surface-bg-alt:#111318;--surface-card:#13151b;--surface-card-alt:#0f1116;--surface-modal:#13151b;--text-primary:#f3f4f6;--text-muted:#b7bcc6;--text-inverse:#0f172a;--accent-primary:#a3a3a3;--accent-primary-hover:#8f8f8f;--accent-border:rgba(255,255,255,.10);
  5226: }
  5227: 
  5228: 
  5229: /* ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  5230:    SIDEBAR SCROLL FIX v3 — definitive
  5231:    ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  5232: 
  5233:    Root cause (confirmed):
  5234:    Setting height:100% on a grid item that already uses the
  5235:    default align-self:stretch causes Chrome to resolve it as
  5236:    100% of the GRID CONTAINER height — not the track height.
  5237:    This makes .side taller than its track, so .nav never
  5238:    overflows it and overflow-y:auto never activates.
  5239: 
  5240:    Correct technique:
  5241:    Do NOT set height:100% on .side. Instead, set overflow:hidden.
  5242:    overflow:hidden on a grid item creates a new BFC and PREVENTS
  5243:    the flex container from growing past its grid track boundary.
  5244:    Now .side's intrinsic height = the track height (definite).
  5245:    flex:1 1 0px on .nav then correctly fills the remaining
  5246:    bounded space and overflow-y:auto activates when content
  5247:    exceeds that space.
  5248: 
  5249:    Specificity note:
  5250:    Line ~2318 has: body:not(.sidebar-collapsed) .side .nav
  5251:    { display:block } at specificity 0-3-1.
  5252:    We must use an equal or higher specificity selector below,
  5253:    placed later in the file, to override it.
  5254:    ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */
  5255: 
  5256: /* 1 ── Sidebar shell: flex column, bounded by overflow:hidden
  5257:         DO NOT add height:100% — grid stretch + overflow:hidden
  5258:         is what actually constrains the height. */
  5259: aside.side,
  5260: .side.card.pad {
  5261:   display: flex !important;
  5262:   flex-direction: column !important;
  5263:   overflow: hidden !important;
  5264:   min-height: 0 !important;
  5265: }
  5266: 
  5267: /* 2 ── Nav: fills remaining space and scrolls.
  5268:         Selector specificity 0-3-1 beats the display:block rule
  5269:         at line ~2318. !important ensures it sticks. */
  5270: body:not(.sidebar-collapsed) .side #nav,
  5271: body:not(.sidebar-collapsed) .side .nav {
  5272:   display: flex !important;
  5273:   flex-direction: column !important;
  5274:   flex: 1 1 0px !important;
  5275:   min-height: 0 !important;
  5276:   overflow-y: auto !important;

  9742: 
  9743: /* ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  9744:    BOTTOM BAR SPACER — v4 PERMANENT FIX (ResizeObserver-driven)
  9745:    ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  9746:    AUTHORITATIVE DRIVER: app.js → _initBarObservers() + _commitBarHeights()
  9747:    Uses ResizeObserver to measure the ACTUAL pixel height of each bar in
  9748:    real-time and writes to <html> inline style (highest specificity):
  9749:      document.documentElement.style.setProperty('--mums-dock-h',      actualQlHeight + 'px')
  9750:      document.documentElement.style.setProperty('--mums-onlinebar-h', actualOnlineHeight + 'px')
  9751: 
  9752:    WHY THIS IS THE PERMANENT FIX:
  9753:    • Previous bug: hardcoded 86px / 44px were SMALLER than actual rendered heights
  9754:      (bars include padding + border → real height was ~57px online, ~86px ql).
  9755:      Grid row 3 spacer was too short → sidebars/main bled under the fixed bars.
  9756:    • ResizeObserver fires whenever bar size changes (content, theme, window resize).
  9757:    • Inline style on <html> cannot be beaten by any CSS :root or theme override.
  9758: 
  9759:    CSS RULES BELOW: animation/visibility only — NO layout values.
  9760:    Do NOT add --mums-dock-h or --mums-onlinebar-h overrides here.
  9761:    ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */
  9762: 
  9763: /* Smooth grid resize when bars toggle — transition on .app row changes */
  9764: .app {
  9765:   transition: grid-template-rows .28s cubic-bezier(.4,0,.2,1);
  9766: }
  9767: 
  9768: /* Online bar bottom anchor is set by JS inline style.
  9769:    CSS fallback only (fires before JS runs): sit at bottom:0 */
  9770: .online-users-bar {
  9771:   transition: bottom .28s cubic-bezier(.4,0,.2,1),
  9772:               transform .28s cubic-bezier(.4,0,.2,1),
  9773:               opacity .22s ease;
  9774: }
  9775: 
  9776: 
  9777: /* ── PERMANENT FIX: Bottom bars must not overlap left/right sidebars ──────────
  9778:    ROOT CAUSE: .online-users-bar and .quicklinks-bar use left:0;right:0 (full viewport width).
  9779:    This causes them to visually overlap the left sidebar (244px) and right sidebar (296px).
  9780:    FIX: Introduce CSS variables for sidebar widths, driven by body classes.
  9781:    These vars are updated automatically when sidebars collapse/expand/hide.
  9782:    ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
  9783: 
  9784: :root {
  9785:   --sidebar-left-w: 245px;  /* 244px left sidebar + 1px grid gap */
  9786:   --sidebar-right-w: 297px; /* 296px right sidebar + 1px grid gap */
  9787: }
  9788: 
  9789: /* Sidebar collapsed: left becomes 72px + 1px gap */
  9790: body.sidebar-collapsed {
  9791:   --sidebar-left-w: 73px;
  9792: }
  9793: 
  9794: /* Rightbar collapsed/hidden: right becomes 0 */
  9795: body.rightbar-collapsed {
  9796:   --sidebar-right-w: 0px;
  9797: }
  9798: 
  9799: /* Apply offset to both fixed bottom bars — FULL WIDTH, edge to edge */
  9800: .online-users-bar,
  9801: .quicklinks-bar {
  9802:   left: 0 !important;
  9803:   right: 0 !important;
  9804:   transition:
  9805:     left .25s cubic-bezier(.4,0,.2,1),
  9806:     right .25s cubic-bezier(.4,0,.2,1),
  9807:     bottom .28s cubic-bezier(.4,0,.2,1),
  9808:     transform .28s cubic-bezier(.4,0,.2,1),
  9809:     opacity .22s ease;
  9810: }
  9811: 
  9812: /* Clocks and quicklinks inner are positioned absolutely inside the bar.
  9813:    Their left/right references are already relative to the bar — no extra offset needed. */
  9814: 
  9815: /* ── Responsive: on narrow screens sidebars collapse, bars go full width ── */
  9816: @media (max-width: 768px) {
  9817:   :root {
  9818:     --sidebar-left-w: 0px;
  9819:     --sidebar-right-w: 0px;
  9820:   }
  9821: }
  9822:

  9827:    ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */
  9828:

=== SECTION 6: SUPABASE ===
=== FILE: supabase/schema.sql ===
     1: -- MUMS Supabase schema (Vercel-ready)
     2: -- Run in Supabase SQL editor.
     3: 
     4: -- 1) Profiles (authoritative user directory)
     5: create table if not exists public.mums_profiles (
     6:   user_id uuid primary key,
     7:   username text unique not null,
     8:   name text not null,
     9:   role text not null check (role in ('SUPER_ADMIN','TEAM_LEAD','ADMIN','MEMBER')),
    10:   team_id text,
    11:   duty text default '',
    12:   created_at timestamptz default now(),
    13:   updated_at timestamptz default now()
    14: );
    15: 
    16: create or replace function public.set_updated_at()
    17: returns trigger language plpgsql as $$
    18: begin
    19:   new.updated_at = now();
    20:   return new;
    21: end $$;
    22: 
    23: drop trigger if exists trg_profiles_updated_at on public.mums_profiles;
    24: create trigger trg_profiles_updated_at
    25: before update on public.mums_profiles
    26: for each row execute function public.set_updated_at();
    27: 
    28: -- 2) Cloud-global mailbox override
    29: create table if not exists public.mums_mailbox_override (
    30:   scope text not null check (scope in ('global','superadmin')),
    31:   enabled boolean not null default false,
    32:   is_frozen boolean not null default true,
    33:   override_iso text not null default '',
    34:   updated_by uuid,
    35:   updated_at timestamptz default now(),
    36:   primary key (scope)
    37: );
    38: 
    39: drop trigger if exists trg_mailbox_override_updated_at on public.mums_mailbox_override;
    40: create trigger trg_mailbox_override_updated_at
    41: before update on public.mums_mailbox_override
    42: for each row execute function public.set_updated_at();
    43: 
    44: insert into public.mums_mailbox_override (scope, enabled, is_frozen, override_iso)
    45: values ('global', false, true, ''), ('superadmin', false, true, '')
    46: on conflict (scope) do nothing;
    47: 
    48: -- --------------------
    49: -- RLS / Policies
    50: -- --------------------
    51: alter table public.mums_profiles enable row level security;
    52: alter table public.mums_mailbox_override enable row level security;
    53: 
    54: -- SECURITY DEFINER helper avoids recursive RLS evaluation inside policies.
    55: create or replace function public.mums_is_super_admin(p_uid uuid)
    56: returns boolean
    57: language sql
    58: stable
    59: security definer
    60: set search_path = public, auth, extensions
    61: as $$
    62:   select exists (
    63:     select 1
    64:     from public.mums_profiles p
    65:     where p.user_id = p_uid
    66:       and p.role = 'SUPER_ADMIN'
    67:   );
    68: $$;
    69: 
    70: -- Allow users to read their own profile
    71: drop policy if exists "profiles_select_own" on public.mums_profiles;
    72: create policy "profiles_select_own" on public.mums_profiles
    73: for select to authenticated
    74: using (auth.uid() = user_id);
    75: 
    76: -- Allow SUPER_ADMIN to read all profiles (for UI directory)
    77: drop policy if exists "profiles_select_superadmin" on public.mums_profiles;
    78: create policy "profiles_select_superadmin" on public.mums_profiles
    79: for select to authenticated
    80: using (public.mums_is_super_admin(auth.uid()));
    81: 
    82: -- Mailbox override: any authenticated user can read global override
    83: drop policy if exists "override_select_auth" on public.mums_mailbox_override;
    84: create policy "override_select_auth" on public.mums_mailbox_override
    85: for select to authenticated
    86: using (true);
    87: 
    88: -- Mailbox override: only SUPER_ADMIN can update
    89: drop policy if exists "override_update_superadmin" on public.mums_mailbox_override;
    90: create policy "override_update_superadmin" on public.mums_mailbox_override
    91: for update to authenticated
    92: using (public.mums_is_super_admin(auth.uid()))
    93: with check (public.mums_is_super_admin(auth.uid()));
    94: 
    95: -- NOTE: Presence tables are created by the app; you may optionally enable RLS there too.
    96: 
    97: -- =====================================================================
    98: -- ADDITIONS (Realtime collaboration)
    99: -- =====================================================================
   100: 
   101: -- Ensure role constraint supports SUPER_USER as well.
   102: alter table if exists public.mums_profiles
   103:   drop constraint if exists mums_profiles_role_check;
   104: alter table if exists public.mums_profiles
   105:   add constraint mums_profiles_role_check
   106:   check (role in ('SUPER_ADMIN', 'SUPER_USER', 'ADMIN', 'TEAM_LEAD', 'MEMBER'));
   107: 
   108: -- Presence table (used for online user overlay). Keep RLS disabled.
   109: create table if not exists public.mums_presence (
   110:   client_id text primary key,
   111:   user_id text not null,
   112:   name text,
   113:   role text,
   114:   team_id text,
   115:   route text,
   116:   last_seen timestamptz not null default now()
   117: );
   118: create index if not exists mums_presence_last_seen_idx on public.mums_presence (last_seen desc);
   119: 
   120: 
   121: -- Collaborative documents store (server-managed; clients pull via /api/sync/*)
   122: create table if not exists public.mums_documents (
   123:   key text primary key,
   124:   value jsonb not null default '{}'::jsonb,
   125:   updated_at timestamptz not null default now(),
   126:   updated_by_user_id uuid,
   127:   updated_by_name text,
   128:   updated_by_client_id text
   129: );
   130: create index if not exists mums_documents_updated_at_idx on public.mums_documents (updated_at desc);
   131: 
   132: -- Maintain updated_at on updates.
   133: create or replace function public.mums_set_updated_at()
   134: returns trigger as $$
   135: begin
   136:   new.updated_at := now();
   137:   return new;
   138: end;
   139: $$ language plpgsql;
   140: 
   141: drop trigger if exists mums_documents_set_updated_at on public.mums_documents;
   142: create trigger mums_documents_set_updated_at
   143: before update on public.mums_documents
   144: for each row execute function public.mums_set_updated_at();
   145: 
   146: -- Secure documents from direct client access (server functions use SERVICE ROLE and bypass RLS).
   147: alter table public.mums_documents enable row level security;
   148: 
   149: -- Authenticated users may read (global read).
   150: drop policy if exists "mums_documents_read" on public.mums_documents;
   151: create policy "mums_documents_read" on public.mums_documents
   152: for select to authenticated using (true);
   153: 
   154: -- No insert/update/delete policies -> denied by default for anon/authenticated.

=== FILE: supabase/schema_update_v2.sql ===
     1: -- MUMS incremental schema update (2026-01-25)
     2: -- Safe to run on an existing Supabase project.
     3: 
     4: -- Expand role constraint to include SUPER_USER.
     5: alter table if exists public.mums_profiles
     6:   drop constraint if exists mums_profiles_role_check;
     7: alter table if exists public.mums_profiles
     8:   add constraint mums_profiles_role_check
     9:   check (role in ('SUPER_ADMIN','SUPER_USER','ADMIN','TEAM_LEAD','MEMBER'));
    10: 
    11: -- Presence (online users)
    12: create table if not exists public.mums_presence(
    13:   client_id text primary key,
    14:   user_id text not null,
    15:   name text,
    16:   role text,
    17:   team_id text,
    18:   route text,
    19:   last_seen timestamptz not null default now()
    20: );
    21: create index if not exists mums_presence_last_seen_idx on public.mums_presence(last_seen desc);
    22: 
    23: -- Collaborative documents store (shared state sync)
    24: create table if not exists public.mums_documents(
    25:   key text primary key,
    26:   value jsonb not null default '{}'::jsonb,
    27:   updated_at timestamptz not null default now(),
    28:   updated_by_user_id uuid null,
    29:   updated_by_name text null,
    30:   updated_by_client_id text null
    31: );
    32: create index if not exists mums_documents_updated_at_idx on public.mums_documents(updated_at);
    33: 
    34: alter table public.mums_documents enable row level security;
    35: 
    36: -- Allow authenticated users to read documents (writes go through server-side API only).
    37: drop policy if exists mums_documents_select_authenticated on public.mums_documents;
    38: create policy mums_documents_select_authenticated
    39:   on public.mums_documents
    40:   for select
    41:   to authenticated
    42:   using (true);
    43: 
    44: -- Ensure Supabase Realtime replication is enabled for mums_documents.
    45: -- (If already enabled, this is a no-op.)
    46: do $$
    47: begin
    48:   execute 'alter publication supabase_realtime add table public.mums_documents';
    49: exception when others then
    50:   -- Ignore errors (publication missing, already added, insufficient privileges).
    51:   null;
    52: end $$;

=== FILE: .env.example ===
     1: # =============================================================================
     2: # NEWMACE Environment Variables
     3: # Phase 637 | QBTabBugFix
     4: # =============================================================================
     5: # HOW THIS WORKS:
     6: #   - Same codebase deploys to BOTH PROD and UAT.
     7: #   - The ONLY thing that changes between environments is these env vars.
     8: #   - Set these in your hosting platform (Cloudflare Pages or Vercel) dashboard.
     9: #   - NEVER commit real values to git.
    10: # =============================================================================
    11: 
    12: # ------------------------------------------------------------
    13: # SUPABASE (Required)
    14: # ------------------------------------------------------------
    15: # PROD:  Use your Production Supabase project values
    16: # UAT:   Use your UAT Supabase project values (separate project)
    17: 
    18: SUPABASE_URL=
    19: SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=
    20: 
    21: # Supabase service role key — server-side only. NEVER expose client-side.
    22: SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=
    23: SUPABASE_DB_POOLER_URL=       # transaction pooler, port 6543
    24: 
    25: # ------------------------------------------------------------
    26: # APP CONFIG (Required)
    27: # ------------------------------------------------------------
    28: # Username-to-email domain for CloudAuth login + user creation
    29: # Example: meys.gov.ph | mums.local
    30: USERNAME_EMAIL_DOMAIN=mums.local
    31: 
    32: # Bootstrap SUPER_ADMIN account (set on first deploy, optional after)
    33: # SUPERADMIN_EMAIL=someone@mums.local
    34: SUPERADMIN_EMAIL=
    35: 
    36: # ------------------------------------------------------------
    37: # STORAGE (Profile photos)
    38: # ------------------------------------------------------------
    39: # Bucket must be PUBLIC in Supabase Storage dashboard.
    40: SUPABASE_PUBLIC_BUCKET=public
    41: 
    42: # ------------------------------------------------------------
    43: # REALTIME & SYNC
    44: # FIX v3.9.30: Increased reconcile interval to 90s (was 15s).
    45: # 15s caused excessive DB reads under 30-user free-tier load.
    46: # ------------------------------------------------------------
    47: SYNC_ENABLE_SUPABASE_REALTIME=true
    48: SYNC_RECONCILE_MS=90000
    49: SYNC_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_MS=7000
    50: SYNC_PULL_RATE_PER_10S=1
    51: SYNC_PUSH_RATE_PER_5S=1
    52: AUTH_CACHE_TTL_MS=300000
    53: CIRCUIT_BREAKER_THRESHOLD=5
    54: 
    55: # ------------------------------------------------------------
    56: # PRESENCE
    57: # FIX v3.9.30: Corrected presence intervals for free tier.
    58: # PRESENCE_POLL_MS=3000 was dangerously low —
    59: # 3s heartbeat × 30 users = 900 writes/min = IO budget destroyed.
    60: # Correct values: heartbeat every 45s, roster list every 90s.
    61: # ------------------------------------------------------------
    62: PRESENCE_POLL_MS=45000
    63: PRESENCE_LIST_POLL_MS=90000
    64: PRESENCE_TTL_SECONDS=120
    65: 
    66: # ------------------------------------------------------------
    67: # E2E / Playwright Tests
    68: # ------------------------------------------------------------
    69: # Store real values as GitHub Secrets. Never commit credentials.
    70: 
    71: # Deployed base URLs
    72: CLOUDFLARE_URL=
    73: VERCEL_URL=
    74: 
    75: # Supabase project ref validation (optional safety check in CI)
    76: EXPECTED_PROD_REF=
    77: EXPECTED_UAT_REF=
    78: 
    79: # Test users (separate accounts per environment)
    80: PROD_USER_EMAIL=
    81: PROD_USER_PASSWORD=
    82: UAT_USER_EMAIL=
    83: UAT_USER_PASSWORD=
